**Blank** function $ L(t) $ that represents the LED light output needed to maintain exactly 500 lumens of total illumination in the living room is derived from the total illumination equation:

$$
\text{Total Illumination} = N(t) + L(t) = 500
$$

Given the natural light function:

$$
N(t) = 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

We can solve for $ L(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right)
$$
$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function ensures that the LED light output compensates for the natural light to maintain a constant total illumination of 500 lumens in the living room.

---

**Blank** total energy consumption of the LEDs over a 24-hour period is calculated by integrating the power consumption over time. The LEDs consume power at a rate of 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. Thus, the total energy consumed is:

$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} 0.1 \cdot L(t) \, dt = 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) dt
$$
$$
= 0.1 \left[ \int_0^{24} 200 \, dt - \int_0^{24} 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) dt \right]
$$
$$
= 0.1 \left[ 200 \cdot 24 - 200 \int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) dt \right]
$$
